name: threat-patch-self-analysis
prompt: Can you threat model the skill at skills/.experimental/threat-patch/? It parses Codex security finding CSVs and generates code patches. I'm worried about what happens if someone feeds it a malicious CSV or if the patch workflow has unintended side effects.
expected_output: Evidence-backed threat model with data flow traces through the CSV parsing and patching workflow.
assertions: Contains at least one data flow trace showing CSV field value → through parse-findings.sh or agent workflow → to a sink operation
Identifies the prompt injection chain: CSV description field → agent context → influences patch generation → source code modification
Notes hooks.json as an existing mitigation AND identifies its limitation (echo-only, not blocking)
Groups related findings if 3+ share a root cause (e.g., multiple untrusted-CSV-field findings clustered)
If exploit chains are found, documents them with prerequisite arrows and terminal impact
Output includes an explicit out-of-scope section
Calibration adjusts severity based on deployment context (interactive vs automated/CI)
